clues.title: The Missing Key
clues.description: The room was locked from the inside, but the key is not in the lock or on the body. It is found wedged beneath the floorboards near the window — placed there deliberately, not dropped.

clues.title: The Poison Vial
clues.description: A small glass vial found in the dead agent's coat pocket. The residue is nightshade extract — lethal in the dose indicated by the staining. But the agent's lips show no trace of the poison. He did not drink it. Someone administered it another way.

clues.title: Agent Marsh's Case Notes
clues.description: A coded notebook found hidden in the lining of the dead agent's coat. The Lamplighter cipher is familiar to you — or can be broken with effort. The notes document six weeks of surveillance on the Gasworks district, tracking shipments of 'anomalous materials' moving through Court of Smoke channels.

clues.title: Ritual Marks on the Body
clues.description: Beneath the agent's shirt, drawn on his chest in consecrated chalk, is a Grey Dawn binding sigil. It was not there when he dressed that morning — someone drew it on him after death. The sigil is designed to prevent a soul from passing beyond the Veil. Someone wanted to keep Agent Marsh's spirit anchored.

clues.title: Gasworks Shipping Manifest
clues.description: A manifest from the Gasworks loading dock listing shipments of 'industrial solvents' to addresses that do not exist. The quantities are wrong — far more than any legitimate operation would require. The manifest is signed by the Gasworks foreman, a man named Croke.

clues.title: Gasworks Containment Sigils
clues.description: Deep in the Gasworks, behind a locked door, the walls are covered in Grey Dawn containment sigils — not chalk, but etched into the brickwork with acid. Something is being stored here that requires occult containment. The air shimmers with a heat that has nothing to do with the furnaces.

clues.title: Court of Smoke Shipping Records
clues.description: Records obtained from a Court of Smoke intermediary documenting the movement of 'specialty goods' through the Gasworks. The goods are described in coded language, but cross-referencing with Marsh's notes reveals they are occult artifacts — objects imbued with Veil energy, harvested from sites of supernatural activity.

clues.title: The Tainted Artifact
clues.description: A small iron box, cold to the touch, containing a shard of obsidian that pulses with a faint inner light. The shard is a Veil fragment — a piece of the boundary between worlds, somehow extracted and contained. It whispers at the edge of hearing. Prolonged exposure causes hallucinations, paranoia, and eventually madness. The Gasworks workers call it 'the Whisper.'

clues.title: Grey Dawn Informant's Warning
clues.description: A Grey Dawn scholar, contacted through back channels, confirms that the binding sigil on Marsh's body is genuine — and deeply forbidden. 'Whoever drew that sigil was trying to interrogate the dead,' she says. 'They wanted to know what Marsh had discovered. And they have the knowledge to do it.' She will say no more, but her fear is palpable.

clues.title: Foreman Croke's Confession
clues.description: Croke, the Gasworks foreman, breaks under pressure. He was paid by the Court of Smoke to look the other way while shipments moved through his loading dock. He did not know what was in the crates until one broke open and a worker went mad. 'The things in those boxes,' he says, 'they're not natural. They talk to you. They show you things.' He gives you the name of his Court contact.

clues.title: The Rival Agent
clues.description: A second Lamplighter agent — codename Sparrow — was known to have a bitter rivalry with Marsh. Sparrow was in Whitechapel the night of the murder and cannot account for his movements. Damning — but Sparrow was conducting his own surveillance on a different target, and his alibi, once verified, is airtight.

clues.title: The Landlord's Grudge
clues.description: The landlord of Marsh's lodgings had threatened to evict him for unpaid rent. He has a key to the room and a motive. But the landlord was at a temperance meeting across London at the time of death, witnessed by thirty people.

clues.title: Marsh's Final Message
clues.description: Scratched into the windowsill with a fingernail, barely visible: 'CROKE KNOWS. VAULT 7. TELL WREN.' Marsh knew he was dying and left a message for the Lamplighters. Vault 7 is in the Gasworks. Wren is a Lamplighter handler.
